title: [InstCombine] Tighten use constraint in factorization transforms

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions 'mul X, 1 → X' as example but doesn't explicitly cover factorization when one multiply is by identity constant 1. This tests the LHS == A || LHS == B check where mul2 equals operand x after simplification.

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i32 @factorize_mul_by_identity_one(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %x1 = add i32 %y, 1
  %add = mul i32 %x, %x1
  ret i32 %add
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @factorize_mul_by_identity_one(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %mul1 = mul i32 %x, %y
  %mul2 = mul i32 %x, 1
  %add = add i32 %mul1, %mul2
  ret i32 %add
}
=>
define i32 @factorize_mul_by_identity_one(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %x1 = add i32 %y, 1
  %add = mul i32 %x, %x1
  ret i32 %add
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions SimplifyQuery propagation to factorizeMathWithShlOps but doesn't detail NSW flag preservation through the profitability check. This tests that nsw flags are correctly propagated when transformation is deemed profitable.

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i8 @factorize_shl_nsw_profitable_check(i8 %x, i8 %y, i8 %z) {
  %1 = add nsw i8 %x, %y
  %add = shl nsw i8 %1, %z
  ret i8 %add
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i8 @factorize_shl_nsw_profitable_check(i8 %x, i8 %y, i8 %z) {
#0:
  %shl1 = shl nsw i8 %x, %z
  %shl2 = shl nsw i8 %y, %z
  %add = add nsw i8 %shl1, %shl2
  ret i8 %add
}
=>
define i8 @factorize_shl_nsw_profitable_check(i8 %x, i8 %y, i8 %z) {
#0:
  %#1 = add nsw i8 %x, %y
  %add = shl nsw i8 %#1, %z
  ret i8 %add
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.source_program:
define i32 @factorize_add_zero_identity_both_sides(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %z) {
  %mul1 = mul i32 %x, %y
  %add0 = add i32 %x, 0
  %mul2 = mul i32 %add0, %z
  %add = add i32 %mul1, %mul2
  ret i32 %add
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary discusses mul by 1 identity but not add by 0 identity on both sides of factorization. Tests the combined effect where add %x, 0 simplifies to %x (RHS == C || RHS == D check) enabling factorization even without one-use constraint.

alive2.src:
define i32 @factorize_add_zero_identity_both_sides(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %z) {
  %mul1 = mul i32 %x, %y
  %add0 = add i32 %x, 0
  %mul2 = mul i32 %add0, %z
  %add = add i32 %mul1, %mul2
  ret i32 %add
}

source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i32 @factorize_add_zero_identity_both_sides(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %z) {
  %mul21 = add i32 %y, %z
  %add = mul i32 %x, %mul21
  ret i32 %add
}
